article_meta.title: Air Force One returns to Washington area due to minor electrical issue, White House says 1 of 4 | President Donald Trump

transcript.content:
You are a news summarization assistant. Summarize the following article and extract its key facts. Respond ONLY with a valid JSON object — no prose, no markdown fences:

Rules:
- summary must be 150-200 words
- key_facts must contain EXACTLY 5 items
- sources_represented must list every distinct perspective included

Summarize this article:

---
HEADLINE: Air Force One returns to Washington area due to minor electrical issue, White House says 1 of 4 | President Donald Trump
SOURCE: Associated Press

ARTICLE:

Air Force One returns to Washington area due to minor electrical issue, White House says President Donald Trump's plane, Air Force One, returned to Joint Base Andrews about an hour after departing for Switzerland on Tuesday evening. White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt said the decision to return was made after takeoff when the crew aboard Air Force One identified "a minor electrical issue" and, out of an abundance of caution, decided to turn around. Things are unloaded from Air Force One after the plane, carrying President Donald Trump to the World Economic Form in Davos, experienced a minor electrical issue after departure, Tuesday, Jan. 20, 2026, and returned to Joint Base Andrews, Md. Trump will board a second plane to complete the trip. (AP Photo/Evan Vucci) Members of the media exit Air Force One after the plane, carrying President Donald Trump to the World Economic Form in Davos, experienced a minor electrical issue after departure, Tuesday, Jan. 20, 2026, and returned to Joint Base Andrews, Md. Trump will board a second plane to complete the trip. (AP Photo/Evan Vucci) President Donald Trump boards Air Force One for a trip to attend the World Economic Form in Davos, Tuesday, Jan. 20, 2026, at Joint Base Andrews, Md. (AP Photo/Evan Vucci) Air Force One returns to Washington area due to minor electrical issue, White House says 1 of 4 President Donald Trump's plane, Air Force One, returned to Joint Base Andrews about an hour after departing for Switzerland on Tuesday evening. White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt said the decision to return was made after takeoff when the crew aboard Air Force One identified "a minor electrical issue" and, out of an abundance of caution, decided to turn around. {
  "summary": "Air Force One, carrying President Donald Trump, was forced to return to Joint Base Andrews near Washington, D.C., about an hour after takeoff on Tuesday evening due to a minor electrical issue. The aircraft was en route to Switzerland, where President Trump was scheduled to attend the World Economic Forum in Davos. According to White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt, the decision to return was made out of an abundance of caution after the crew identified the issue post-departure. Upon landing, cargo was unloaded from Air Force One, and members of the media exited the aircraft. President Trump is expected to continue his journey to Davos by boarding a secondary plane. No reports of injuries or further complications have been noted, and the incident appears to be entirely precautionary, ensuring the safety of the president and all aboard. The White House's swift response aims to maintain the integrity of the president's schedule while addressing any technical concerns related to Air Force One operation.",

    "President Trump will continue to Davos on a second plane.",
    "No injuries or further complications were reported."
  ],
  "sources_represented": [
    "White House",
    "Associated Press"
  ]
}

parsed_output.summary: Air Force One, carrying President Donald Trump, was forced to return to Joint Base Andrews near Washington, D.C., about an hour after takeoff on Tuesday evening due to a minor electrical issue. The aircraft was en route to Switzerland, where President Trump was scheduled to attend the World Economic Forum in Davos. According to White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt, the decision to return was made out of an abundance of caution after the crew identified the issue post-departure. Upon landing, cargo was unloaded from Air Force One, and members of the media exited the aircraft. President Trump is expected to continue his journey to Davos by boarding a secondary plane. No reports of injuries or further complications have been noted, and the incident appears to be entirely precautionary, ensuring the safety of the president and all aboard. The White House's swift response aims to maintain the integrity of the president's schedule while addressing any technical concerns related to Air Force One operation.
key_facts: Air Force One returned to Joint Base Andrews due to a minor electrical issue.
The incident occurred about an hour after departing for the World Economic Forum in Davos, Switzerland.
White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt stated the decision was made out of an abundance of caution.
President Trump will continue to Davos on a second plane.
No injuries or further complications were reported.
